title: Using Freestyle VM to Create Trails From Mobile App
summary:
**What we pay for.** Freestyle hosts the Git repo and runs a sandboxed VM that clones it in-network; the **VM is the metered cost** — vCPU, memory, and storage billed by the hour — while each imported repo only counts against the plan's repo cap (Free 500 / Hobby 5,000 / Pro 50,000), not a per-repo charge.

**The security boundary.** The user's GitHub token stays on the host and authenticates only the import; the VM gets a separate scoped, read-only token and talks only to `git.freestyle.sh`, never github.com.

**Teardown stops the meter.** The host deletes the VM, identity, and repo after publishing — the VM's hourly meter stops and the repo slot frees, so nothing rented outlives the session.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph GitHub
    ghrepo[(user repo)]
  end
  subgraph Freestyle
    fsrepo[(imported repo)]
    subgraph VM[Freestyle VM]
      vmrepo[(cloned repo)]
    end
  end
  ghrepo -- server-side import --> fsrepo
  fsrepo -- in-network clone --> vmrepo
```

markers.label: Why the import runs server-side
markers.description: Hosted trail authoring imports a user's GitHub repo into a fresh Freestyle Git repo server-side, so a Freestyle VM can clone it in-network. The user's GitHub token stays on the host — it authenticates the import and never enters the VM. Freestyle performs the GitHub fetch on its own network and hosts the result; the VM only ever talks to `git.freestyle.sh`.

markers.label: Importing from Github to Freestyle
markers.description: `freestyle.git.repos.create({ name, source: { url, rev } })` runs host-side and returns a `repoId`. Freestyle fetches the repo from github.com on its own network — the `url` embeds `x-access-token:${userToken}` so private repos resolve, and `rev` selects a branch/tag/sha (`null` → the repo's default branch). Each create consumes one of the plan's repository slots (Free 500 / Hobby 5,000 / Pro 50,000 repos), not a per-repo dollar charge — and teardown returns the slot.

markers.label: Access within Freestyle
markers.description: The host mints a scoped identity with `freestyle.identities.create()`, grants it `read` on just this `repoId` via `ident.permissions.git.grant(...)`, and issues a clone token with `ident.tokens.create()`. The VM receives only this `gitToken`, never the user's GitHub credentials. Identities and tokens are not separately billed.

markers.label: The Freestyle VM clones in-network
markers.description: Inside the Freestyle VM, `git clone --depth 1 https://x-access-token:${gitToken}@git.freestyle.sh/${repoId} /work` pulls the repo in-network from Freestyle Git. The VM reaches only `git.freestyle.sh` with the scoped token — it never touches github.com. The VM is where usage is billed — by vCPU, memory, and storage held per hour — so a short clone-and-run keeps cost low.

markers.label: Tear down everything provisioned
markers.description: Once the agent has run and the result is published on the host, the integration deletes everything it provisioned: `freestyle.vms.delete({ vmId })`, `freestyle.identities.delete({ identityId })`, and `freestyle.git.repos.delete({ repoId })`. Deleting the VM stops its hourly meter and deleting the repo frees its plan slot, so an abandoned session leaves nothing accruing cost.

markers.label: What a session costs
markers.description: Of the three resources a session provisions, only the VM is metered: vCPU at $0.04032/hr, memory at $0.0129/GiB-hr, storage at $0.000086/GiB-hr, billed for as long as the VM is alive. The imported repo counts against the plan's repo cap (Free 500 / Hobby 5,000 / Pro 50,000) rather than a per-repo fee, and identities/tokens are not separately billed — so session cost scales with VM time, not repo count. Figures are a 2026-06-16 snapshot of `freestyle.sh/pricing`.
